item.title: How Reform Plans to Upend Decades of UK Climate Action
item.description: Richard Tice, Reform UK’s deputy leader, wants to revive Britain’s oil and gas industry and cut back on renewables.

item.title: Reform Deputy Leader Tice ‘Fundamentally’ Rejects Climate Science
item.description: In an extensive interview about climate change and energy policy, Richard Tice dismissed the threat of global warming and doubled down on fossil fuels.

item.title: Reform UK’s plan to end decades of climate action
item.description: Reform UK’s plan to end decades of climate action【原文：Reform UK’s plan to end decades of climate action】

item.title: Australia’s Edify Secures Financing for Two Solar-Storage Plants
item.description: Australia’s Edify Energy Pty Ltd. secured financing needed to begin construction on two massive solar-and-battery power plants.

item.title: India Weighs $1 Billion Aid for Private Electric Buses, Trucks
item.description: India is considering incentives exceeding $1 billion to spur private-sector adoption of electric buses and trucks, in an attempt to cut fossil-fuel use in the segment amid a deepening energy crisis, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: UK Asked to ‘Provide Clarity’ Over Flood Insurer of Last Resort
item.description: The UK government must provide clarity on the future of Flood Re or risk seeing its failure to do so feed through to the country’s housing market.

item.title: McDonald’s Won’t Meet Some Emissions Goals, Citing Energy Crunch
item.description: McDonald’s Corp. said it doesn’t expect to reach a goal of reducing emissions from its supply chain and franchised restaurants by 2030, the timeline it had originally laid out.

item.title: Google Executive Says ‘Moonshot’ Clean Energy Goal Is Intact
item.description: A senior executive at Alphabet Inc.’s Google said the company isn’t abandoning plans to run its business on 24/7 carbon-free energy by 2030, even as the buildout of data centers for AI raises questions about the feasibility of such a goal.

item.title: The Worst-Case Climate Scenario Is Dead — and Still Spurring Debate
item.description: RCP8.5 is officially retired, scientists say.

item.title: Scientists Ditch Worst-Case Climate Scenario, With Ridicule From Trump
item.description: Global warming skeptics, including the US president, have celebrated the end of the scenario known as RCP8.5. Experts say it was retired for being outdated, not wrong.

item.title: Newfoundland to Renegotiate Power Deal With Hydro-Quebec
item.description: The leader of Newfoundland and Labrador said an electricity supply agreement reached by the previous provincial government with Quebec is not in his province’s long-term interest, and new negotiations will unfold.

item.title: Boaz Weinstein’s Hedge Fund Takes Aim at Impax’s ‘Terrible’ Run
item.description: As Boaz Weinstein’s hedge fund, Saba Capital Management, takes on one of the most high-profile environmental investment trusts in Britain, the standoff is morphing into a particularly brutal moment for ESG.

item.title: California Residents Flee Fires That Sent Smoke Into Los Angeles
item.description: Choppy winds will continue across Southern California, where firefighters are battling several blazes that have forced some residents to flee their homes and sent smoke into downtown Los Angeles.

item.title: Burgum Dismisses US Oil Export Curbs as ‘Bad on All Accounts’
item.description: Trump administration officials are holding firm against restricting American oil and gasoline exports, even as worries grow about dwindling domestic stockpiles with the world leaning on US crude to fill a shortfall caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: French Grid Keeps Nuclear Reactors Online Despite Solar Surge
item.description: France’s grid operator is keeping extra nuclear reactors online to keep the system stable despite strong solar power generation, contributing to a supply surplus and deeply discounted electricity prices.

item.title: Reform UK Threatens Legal Challenges to National Grid Projects
item.description: Reform UK threatened to use the nation’s courts to delay major electricity grid projects, opening a new political front against Britain’s plans to rapidly expand offshore wind power and modernize the energy system.

item.title: Biggest US Wind Project Is Due to Come Online Next Month
item.description: A wind project billed as the largest clean energy installation in the US is due to come online next month, even as the pipeline of new large-scale renewables dries up amid pressure from the Trump administration.

item.title: Scorching Summer Pushes India’s Power Demand to Record Again
item.description: India’s peak electricity demand climbed to a record for the second consecutive day as blistering heat pushed up use of cooling appliances such as air-conditioners.

item.title: China Aims to Raise 6 Billion Yuan in Hong Kong Green Bond Sale
item.description: China is seeking to raise as much as 6 billion yuan ($882 million) in a green sovereign bond sale in Hong Kong next week, as the nation continues to build its presence in offshore debt markets and aims to fund climate action.

item.title: World Bank Prices $200 Million Catastrophe Bond for Jamaica
item.description: The World Bank has priced a new catastrophe bond designed to help Jamaica cope with the threat of hurricanes.

item.title: Iran Energy Shock Supports Boom in Renewables and Electrification
item.description: Solar power will be the single-largest source of electricity by 2032, BloombergNEF researchers said in their annual New Energy Outlook report.

item.title: To Sell a Home in This California City, It Must Be Climate Friendly
item.description: Berkeley is requiring home sellers to make green upgrades as a condition of sale.

item.title: UK Energy Price Cap Seen Surging 13% in Biggest Gain Since 2023
item.description: The UK’s cap on household energy bills is set to jump 13% this summer as the war in Iran drives up wholesale gas and power costs, adding to broader inflationary pressures weighing on the economy.

item.title: CATL, Tencent Seek to Boost Waning Demand for Carbon Credits
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd., two of China’s most valuable companies, are joining a new initiative intended to bolster corporate demand in the slowing market for carbon credits.

item.title: Global Building Emissions Tick Up as Urbanization Accelerates
item.description: Gains in energy efficiency aren’t enough to offset the impact of new construction as built-up areas expand.

item.title: Hive Hydrogen Says Topsoe to Supply Electrolyzer for South Africa
item.description: Hive Hydrogen South Africa said it’s selected Topsoe A/S to supply it with about $1 billion in equipment for its green ammonia project in the country’s Eastern Cape province.

item.title: Fast Moving Fire Prompts Evacuations Northwest of Los Angeles
item.description: A fast moving brush fire in Simi Valley has prompted evacuations as winds whip the flames out of control.

item.title: US House Unveiled Plan to Charge $130 Fee for Electric Vehicles
item.description: House lawmakers made public a massive transportation bill Sunday that would impose new fees on electric vehicles as gasoline prices surge following the Iran war.

item.title: Why Microsoft’s 24/7 Carbon-Free Pledge Matters for Emissions
item.description: The tech giant and its peers are struggling to meet ambitious climate goal in the AI era.

item.title: Temasek Likely to Miss 2030 Climate Target, CEO Pillay Says
item.description: Singapore’s state-owned investor Temasek Holdings Pte is set to miss its 2030 carbon-emissions target amid global turmoil and artificial intelligence’s rising demand for energy.
